name: Putnam School Employees
description_short: Putnam School Employees Federal Credit Union is a nonprofit cooperative financial institution serving Putnam County public employees and their families since 1972.
description_long: Putnam School Employees Federal Credit Union was founded on November 9, 1972, with a charter to serve employees of the Putnam County Board of Education. The credit union began operations from the home of founding members Mr. and Mrs. Raymond Peak before relocating to space donated by the Putnam County Board of Education, and currently operates from the Putnam Career and Technical Center in Eleanor, West Virginia. As a member-focused credit union, the institution offers traditional banking and lending services including savings accounts, loan products, and online banking capabilities. Members can access loan calculators, review current loan rates and dividend rates, and conduct transactions through their online banking platform. The credit union maintains physical branch services with specific operating hours and dedicated staff available for member assistance. Putnam School Employees Federal Credit Union distinguishes itself through its cooperative ownership structure where members democratically control the institution via an elected volunteer board. Membership has expanded beyond its original scope to include employees of multiple Putnam County organizations including the County Commission, City of Winfield, City of Hurricane, and the Solid Waste Authority, plus their family members. This expansion demonstrates growth while maintaining its mission-driven, member-centric approach. The credit union operates with standard banking practices including a 3-business-day processing requirement for large withdrawals over $50,000 and daily lunch closures. As a small, local credit union, it may have more limited product offerings and technology capabilities compared to larger regional or national financial institutions, though it provides personalized service to its membership base.
services: Loan products (specific types not detailed on website)
Personal member service via phone and email
Membership to family members of eligible employees
pros: Nonprofit cooperative structure means profits return to members as dividends rather than shareholders
Democratic governance with volunteer board elected by members ensures member representation
Expanded membership eligibility to multiple Putnam County organizations and their families
Personalized, friendly service with direct contact via email and phone
Online banking platform available for member convenience
Membership extended to family members of eligible employees
Long-established institution with 50+ years of community service (founded 1972)
cons: Limited to local Eleanor, WV location with no branches outside Putnam County
Requires 3 business days for withdrawal requests exceeding $50,000
Restricted membership eligibility based on employment with specific Putnam County entities
Website lacks transparency on specific loan products, rates, and account features
Limited operating hours with closure during monthly board meetings and daily lunch breaks
best_for: Putnam County public sector employees and their families
Members of Putnam County Commission, City of Winfield, City of Hurricane, and Solid Waste Authority
Consumers seeking local, community-focused banking with member ownership
Those preferring personalized service over large national banking institutions
diagnosis: Best suited for Putnam County public employees and their families seeking local, member-owned banking with personalized service. Primary caveat: membership is restricted to specific employer groups and geographic location, making it inaccessible to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Nonprofit cooperative credit union serving Putnam County public employees and their families since 1972.
